description:
Two-layer command model: fixed phase shortcuts + /aidlc dispatcher, auto-provision on workflow edit

## Summary

Replace the **one-slash-command-per-(pipeline × phase)** model with a **two-layer command model**:

1. **Shortcut layer** — a fixed set of phase commands (`/plan`, `/design`, …) shared across all pipelines.
2. **Backbone dispatcher** — a single `/aidlc <epic> [phase]` that resolves the composition at runtime and drives "next eligible phase".

Both layers resolve composition via **`epic → its pipeline → phase`**, never by command name. So two pipelines that reuse the same phase name (with different agent/skill wiring) never share composition — they are disambiguated by which pipeline the epic is bound to.

## Current behavior

`.claude/commands/` after installing `sdlc-parallel-full`:

```
sdlc-parallel-full-plan.md
sdlc-parallel-full-design.md
sdlc-parallel-full-test-plan.md
sdlc-parallel-full-implement.md
sdlc-parallel-full-generate-test-cases.md
sdlc-parallel-full-execute-test.md
```

Id scheme — `pipelineCommandId()` in `packages/core/src/presets/builtinWorkflows.ts`:

```ts
// `<pipelineId>-<phaseId>` (e.g. `sdlc-parallel-full-plan`). Keying by pipeline
// keeps commands distinct when multiple pipelines reuse the same phase names.
export function pipelineCommandId(pipelineId: string, phaseId: string): string {
  return `${pipelineId}-${phaseId}`;
}
```

`N` phases × `M` pipelines = `N·M` command files, each a full ~10–15 KB composition (persona + phase skill + task). `workspace.yaml` mirrors the same fan-out under `slash_commands:`.

## Why the pipeline prefix is unnecessary

The phase set is **already canonical and shared** — `PHASES` in `packages/core/src/presets/builtinWorkflows.ts:98`, and the comment states *"All presets share the same 9-phase shape."* What differs per pipeline is only the **wiring** (which agent/skill/model runs a phase), not the phase set. Real pipelines (e.g. `GH-68` in `workspace.yaml`) are subsets/reorderings of the canonical phases, not novel phases.

Because composition can be resolved at runtime from the epic's pipeline binding, encoding the pipeline into the command **name** is redundant. The `<pipeline>-<phase>` namespacing solves the same-phase-name collision that runtime resolution already solves.

## Proposed design — two layers

### Layer 1 — Shortcut commands (fixed set)

One command per canonical phase, shared across pipelines, no pipeline prefix:

```
/plan <epic>
/design <epic>
/test-plan <epic>
/implement <epic>
/unit-test <epic>          # NEW — split out from implement's ['implement','unit-test'] skills
/benchmark <epic>          # NEW — performance / benchmark run
/generate-test-cases <epic>
/execute-test <epic>
```

- Count = fixed `N` (number of canonical phases), independent of how many pipelines exist. Adding a pipeline adds **zero** commands.
- `/design GH-70` resolves `GH-70 → its pipeline → the `design` wiring of that pipeline`. Two pipelines with different `design` wiring both work, no shared composition.
- Shortcuts are thin aliases into the backbone dispatcher (below).

**Note on `unit-test` / `benchmark`:** these are commonly-run activities that today are folded into `implement` (skills `['implement','unit-test']`) or not modeled at all. Adding them as first-class shortcuts is desired. Open detail: when a phase isn't a distinct step in the epic's pipeline, the shortcut should either run the corresponding skill portion or no-op with a clear message — to be decided.

### Layer 2 — Backbone dispatcher

A single generic command:

```
/aidlc <epic> [phase]
```

- **No phase arg** → read the epic run state (`docs/epics/<epic>/state.json`), run the **next eligible phase** (respecting `depends_on` + gates). This is the primary way to drive a pipeline.
- **Explicit phase** → run that phase, validated against the pipeline DAG. Also the escape hatch for any phase that has no shortcut command.
- Loads the agent+skill composition at runtime from the pipeline definition instead of pre-writing one file per (pipeline × phase).

### Auto-provision commands on workflow create/edit

When the user **creates a new workflow** or **edits an existing one**, scan its steps. For any phase that has **no corresponding command yet** (e.g. a custom phase like `security-review` not in the canonical set), show a popup:

> "These phases in your workflow don't have a command in AIDLC yet: `security-review`, `data-migration`. Create commands for them so you can run them?" → **[Create commands]** / [Skip]

On confirm, generate the shortcut command(s) for those phases so they behave like the built-in ones. This makes the fixed shortcut set **extensible** without hand-editing `.claude/commands/`, and keeps the backbone dispatcher as the always-available fallback for anything not provisioned.

## Benefits

- `N` fixed shortcut commands + 1 backbone, instead of `N·M`.
- Palette/`/`-menu stays small and stable as pipelines are added.
- Persona/skill composition resolved once at runtime — no duplicated large files on disk.
- "Run next step" is a first-class default (`/aidlc <epic>`).
- Custom phases get real commands via the create/edit popup instead of breaking the model.

## Constraints / open questions

- **`epic → pipeline` must be 1-1 at any point in time** — the dispatcher resolves wiring from the epic's bound pipeline, so an epic that could run under multiple pipelines is ambiguous. Needs a rule for recipe/pipeline switching mid-run.
- Exact behavior of `/unit-test` and `/benchmark` when the epic's pipeline has no such distinct step (run the skill vs. no-op).
- **Back-compat:** keep generating the per-phase `<pipeline>-<phase>` files behind a flag, or migrate existing workspaces' `slash_commands:` on upgrade?
- Argument completion for the `[phase]` arg so discoverability isn't lost.

## Touch points

- `pipelineCommandId()` + preset composition — `packages/core/src/presets/builtinWorkflows.ts` (`PHASES` at :98, export at :583).
- `slash_commands:` generation in the preset/workspace writers.
- Workflow create/edit wizards (popup + auto-provision) — `packages/extension/src/v2/presetWizards.ts`, `workspaceCommands.ts`, `workspaceWebview.ts`.
- Run-dispatch (`runWithFeedback` / "Mark step done") in `packages/extension/src/v2/workspaceCommands.ts` — send `/aidlc <epic> <phase>` instead of `<slash> <epic>`.

## Scope

- [ ] Fixed shortcut command set for canonical phases (incl. `unit-test`, `benchmark`).
- [ ] Backbone `/aidlc <epic> [phase]` dispatcher; default to next-eligible phase.
- [ ] Runtime composition resolution via `epic → pipeline → phase`.
- [ ] Popup + auto-provision of commands for phases missing one, on workflow create/edit.
- [ ] Update `slash_commands:` generation and extension run-dispatch.
- [ ] Back-compat decision for existing per-phase commands.
